content: Just as the language teaching definition of reflexion frames it as a deliberate metacognitive practice for human learners to consolidate learning by reflecting on output, the original Reflexion agent framework for LLMs can be enhanced by borrowing structured human reflexion routines from second-language writing pedagogy to improve LLM self-reflection mechanisms. This creates a bidirectional improvement cycle: pedagogical reflexion scaffolds give structure to unstructured LLM self-reflection, while LLM reflexion research provides new empirical data to refine how we teach metacognitive reflexion to human students.
